1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم والصافات صفا
1.english: By those [angels] lined up in rows
And those who drive [the clouds]
And those who recite the message,
Indeed, your God is One,

1.arabic: رب السماوات والأرض وما بينهما ورب المشارق
1.english: Lord of the heavens and the earth and that between them and Lord of the sunrises.
1.transliteration: Rabbus samaawaati wal ardi wa maa bainahumaa wa Rabbul mashaariq

1.arabic: إنا زينا السماء الدنيا بزينة الكواكب
1.english: Indeed, We have adorned the nearest heaven with an adornment of stars
1.transliteration: Innaa zaiyannas samaaa 'ad dunyaa bizeenatinil kawaakib

1.english: And as protection against every rebellious devil
1.transliteration: Wa hifzam min kulli Shaitaanim maarid

1.arabic: لا يسمعون إلى الملإ الأعلى ويقذفون من كل جانب
1.english: [So] they may not listen to the exalted assembly [of angels] and are pelted from every side,
1.transliteration: Laa yassamma 'oona ilal mala il a'alaa wa yuqzafoona min kulli jaanib

1.english: Repelled; and for them is a constant punishment,

1.arabic: إلا من خطف الخطفة فأتبعه شهاب ثاقب
1.english: Except one who snatches [some words] by theft, but they are pursued by a burning flame, piercing [in brightness].
1.transliteration: Illaa man khatifal khatfata fa atba'ahoo shihaabun saaqib

1.arabic: فاستفتهم أهم أشد خلقا أم من خلقنا ۚ إنا خلقناهم من طين لازب
1.english: Then inquire of them, [O Muhammad], "Are they a stronger [or more difficult] creation or those [others] We have created?" Indeed, We created men from sticky clay.
1.transliteration: Fastaftihim ahum ashaddu khalqan am man khalaqnaa; innaa khalaqnaahum min teenil laazib

1.english: But you wonder, while they mock,
And when they are reminded, they remember not.
And when they see a sign, they ridicule
1.transliteration: Wa izaa ra aw Aayatinw yastaskhiroon

1.arabic: وقالوا إن هذا إلا سحر مبين
1.english: And say, "This is not but obvious magic.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalooo in haazaa illaa sihrum mubeen

1.arabic: أإذا متنا وكنا ترابا وعظاما أإنا لمبعوثون
1.english: When we have died and become dust and bones, are we indeed to be resurrected?
1.transliteration: 'A-izaa mitnaa wa kunnaa turaabanw wa 'izaaman 'ainnaa lamab'oosoon

1.english: And our forefathers [as well]?"
Say, "Yes, and you will be [rendered] contemptible."

1.arabic: فإنما هي زجرة واحدة فإذا هم ينظرون
1.english: It will be only one shout, and at once they will be observing.
1.transliteration: Fa innamaa hiya zajra tunw waahidatun fa izaa hum yanzuroon

1.arabic: وقالوا يا ويلنا هذا يوم الدين
1.english: They will say, "O woe to us! This is the Day of Recompense."
1.transliteration: Qa qaaloo yaa wailanaa haazaa Yawmud-Deen

1.arabic: هذا يوم الفصل الذي كنتم به تكذبون
1.english: [They will be told], "This is the Day of Judgement which you used to deny."
1.transliteration: Haazaa Yawmul Faslil lazee kuntum bihee tukaziboon

1.arabic: ۞ احشروا الذين ظلموا وأزواجهم وما كانوا يعبدون
1.english: [The angels will be ordered], "Gather those who committed wrong, their kinds, and what they used to worship
1.transliteration: Uhshurul lazeena zalamoo wa azwaajahum wa maa kaanoo ya'budoon

1.arabic: من دون الله فاهدوهم إلى صراط الجحيم
1.english: Other than Allah, and guide them to the path of Hellfire
1.transliteration: Min doonil laahi fahdoohum ilaa siraatil Jaheem

1.english: And stop them; indeed, they are to be questioned."
[They will be asked], "What is [wrong] with you? Why do you not help each other?"
But they, that Day, are in surrender.
And they will approach one another blaming each other.

1.arabic: قالوا إنكم كنتم تأتوننا عن اليمين
1.english: They will say, "Indeed, you used to come at us from the right."
The oppressors will say, "Rather, you [yourselves] were not believers,

1.arabic: وما كان لنا عليكم من سلطان ۖ بل كنتم قوما طاغين
1.english: And we had over you no authority, but you were a transgressing people.
1.transliteration: Wa maa kaana lanaa 'alaikum min sultaanim bal kuntum qawman taagheen

1.arabic: فحق علينا قول ربنا ۖ إنا لذائقون
1.english: So the word of our Lord has come into effect upon us; indeed, we will taste [punishment].
And we led you to deviation; indeed, we were deviators."
So indeed they, that Day, will be sharing in the punishment.
Indeed, that is how We deal with the criminals.

1.arabic: إنهم كانوا إذا قيل لهم لا إله إلا الله يستكبرون
1.english: Indeed they, when it was said to them, "There is no deity but Allah," were arrogant
1.transliteration: Innahum kaanooo izaa qeela lahum laaa ilaaha illal laahu yastakbiroon

1.arabic: ويقولون أئنا لتاركو آلهتنا لشاعر مجنون
1.english: And were saying, "Are we to leave our gods for a mad poet?"
1.transliteration: Wa yaqooloona a'innaa lataarikooo aalihatinaa lishaa'irim majnoon

1.english: Rather, the Prophet has come with the truth and confirmed the [previous] messengers.
1.transliteration: bal jaaa'a bilhaqqi wa saddaqal mursaleen

1.english: Indeed, you [disbelievers] will be tasters of the painful punishment,

1.arabic: وما تجزون إلا ما كنتم تعملون
1.english: And you will not be recompensed except for what you used to do -
1.transliteration: Wa maa tujzawna illaa maa kuntum ta'maloon

1.english: But not the chosen servants of Allah.
Those will have a provision determined -
Fruits; and they will be honored
In gardens of pleasure
On thrones facing one another.
There will be circulated among them a cup [of wine] from a flowing spring,
White and delicious to the drinkers;

1.arabic: لا فيها غول ولا هم عنها ينزفون
1.english: No bad effect is there in it, nor from it will they be intoxicated.
1.transliteration: Laa feehaa ghawlunw wa laa hum 'anhaa yunzafoon

1.english: And with them will be women limiting [their] glances, with large, [beautiful] eyes,
As if they were [delicate] eggs, well-protected.
And they will approach one another, inquiring of each other.

1.arabic: قال قائل منهم إني كان لي قرين
1.english: A speaker among them will say, "Indeed, I had a companion [on earth]
1.transliteration: Qaala qaaa'ilum minhum innee kaana lee qareen

1.english: Who would say, 'Are you indeed of those who believe

1.arabic: أإذا متنا وكنا ترابا وعظاما أإنا لمدينون
1.english: That when we have died and become dust and bones, we will indeed be recompensed?'"
1.transliteration: 'A-izaa mitnaa wa kunnaa turaabanw wa 'izaaman 'ainnaa lamadeenoon

1.english: He will say, "Would you [care to] look?"
And he will look and see him in the midst of the Hellfire.
1.transliteration: Fattala'a fara aahu fee sawaaa'il Jaheem

1.english: He will say, "By Allah, you almost ruined me.

1.arabic: ولولا نعمة ربي لكنت من المحضرين
1.english: If not for the favor of my Lord, I would have been of those brought in [to Hell].
1.transliteration: Wa law laa ni'matu Rabbee lakuntu minal muhdareen

1.english: Then, are we not to die

1.arabic: إلا موتتنا الأولى وما نحن بمعذبين
1.english: Except for our first death, and we will not be punished?"
1.transliteration: Illa mawtatanal oola wa maa nahnu bimu'azzabeen

1.english: Indeed, this is the great attainment.
For the like of this let the workers [on earth] work.

1.arabic: أذلك خير نزلا أم شجرة الزقوم
1.english: Is Paradise a better accommodation or the tree of zaqqum?
1.transliteration: Azaalika khairun nuzulan am shajaratuz Zaqqom

1.english: Indeed, We have made it a torment for the wrongdoers.

1.arabic: إنها شجرة تخرج في أصل الجحيم
1.english: Indeed, it is a tree issuing from the bottom of the Hellfire,
1.transliteration: Innahaa shajaratun takhruju feee aslil Jaheem

1.english: Its emerging fruit as if it was heads of the devils.

1.arabic: فإنهم لآكلون منها فمالئون منها البطون
1.english: And indeed, they will eat from it and fill with it their bellies.
1.transliteration: Fa innahum la aakiloona minhaa famaali'oona minhal butoon

1.arabic: ثم إن لهم عليها لشوبا من حميم
1.english: Then indeed, they will have after it a mixture of scalding water.
1.transliteration: Summa inna lahum 'alaihaa lashawbam min hameem

1.english: Then indeed, their return will be to the Hellfire.
1.transliteration: Summa inna marji'ahum la ilal Jaheem

1.english: Indeed they found their fathers astray.
So they hastened [to follow] in their footsteps.
And there had already strayed before them most of the former peoples,
1.transliteration: Wa laqad dalla qablahum aksarul awwaleen

1.english: And We had already sent among them warners.
Then look how was the end of those who were warned -
But not the chosen servants of Allah.
And Noah had certainly called Us, and [We are] the best of responders.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad naadaanaa Noohun falani'mal mujeeboon

1.english: And We saved him and his family from the great affliction.
1.transliteration: Wa jajainaahu wa ahlahoo minal karbil 'azeem

1.english: And We made his descendants those remaining [on the earth]
And left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:
"Peace upon Noah among the worlds."
Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.
Then We drowned the disbelievers.
And indeed, among his kind was Abraham,
1.transliteration: Wa ina min shee'atihee la Ibraaheem

1.english: When he came to his Lord with a sound heart
1.transliteration: Iz jaaa'a Rabbahoo bi qalbin saleem

1.arabic: إذ قال لأبيه وقومه ماذا تعبدون
1.english: [And] when he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?
1.transliteration: Iz qaala li abeehi wa qawmihee maazaa ta'budoon

1.english: Is it falsehood [as] gods other than Allah you desire?
Then what is your thought about the Lord of the worlds?"
And he cast a look at the stars
And said, "Indeed, I am [about to be] ill."
So they turned away from him, departing.

1.arabic: فراغ إلى آلهتهم فقال ألا تأكلون
1.english: Then he turned to their gods and said, "Do you not eat?
1.transliteration: Faraagha ilaaa aalihatihim faqaala alaa taakuloon

1.english: What is [wrong] with you that you do not speak?"
And he turned upon them a blow with [his] right hand.
Then the people came toward him, hastening.
He said, "Do you worship that which you [yourselves] carve,
While Allah created you and that which you do?"

1.arabic: قالوا ابنوا له بنيانا فألقوه في الجحيم
1.english: They said, "Construct for him a furnace and throw him into the burning fire."
1.transliteration: Qaalub noo lahoo bun yaanan fa alqoohu fil jaheem

1.english: And they intended for him a plan, but We made them the most debased.
1.transliteration: Fa araadoo bihee kaidan faja 'alnaahumul asfaleen

1.arabic: وقال إني ذاهب إلى ربي سيهدين
1.english: And [then] he said, "Indeed, I will go to [where I am ordered by] my Lord; He will guide me.
1.transliteration: Wa qaala innee zaahibun ilaa Rabbee sa yahdeen

1.english: My Lord, grant me [a child] from among the righteous."
So We gave him good tidings of a forbearing boy.

1.arabic: فلما بلغ معه السعي قال يا بني إني أرى في المنام أني أذبحك فانظر ماذا ترى ۚ قال يا أبت افعل ما تؤمر ۖ ستجدني إن شاء الله من الصابرين
1.english: And when he reached with him [the age of] exertion, he said, "O my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I [must] sacrifice you, so see what you think." He said, "O my father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, of the steadfast."
1.transliteration: Falamma balagha ma'a hus sa'ya qaala yaa buniya inneee araa fil manaami anneee azbahuka fanzur maazaa taraa; qaala yaaa abatif 'al maa tu'maru satajidunee in shaaa'allaahu minas saabireen

1.english: And when they had both submitted and he put him down upon his forehead,
We called to him, "O Abraham,

1.arabic: قد صدقت الرؤيا ۚ إنا كذلك نجزي المحسنين
1.english: You have fulfilled the vision." Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
1.transliteration: Qad saddaqtar ru'yaa; innaa kazaalika najzil muhsineen

1.english: Indeed, this was the clear trial.
And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,
And We left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:
"Peace upon Abraham."
Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.
And We gave him good tidings of Isaac, a prophet from among the righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa bashsharnaahu bi Ishaaqa Nabiyayam minas saaliheen

1.arabic: وباركنا عليه وعلى إسحاق ۚ ومن ذريتهما محسن وظالم لنفسه مبين
1.english: And We blessed him and Isaac. But among their descendants is the doer of good and the clearly unjust to himself.
1.transliteration: Wa baaraknaa 'alaihi wa 'alaaa Ishaaq; wa min zurriyya tihimaa muhsinunw wa zaalimul linafshihee mubeen

1.english: And We did certainly confer favor upon Moses and Aaron.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad mananna alaa Moosaa wa Haaroon

1.english: And We saved them and their people from the great affliction,
1.transliteration: Wa najjainaahumaa wa qawmahumaa minal karbil 'azeem

1.english: And We supported them so it was they who overcame.
And We gave them the explicit Scripture,
And We guided them on the straight path.
And We left for them [favorable mention] among later generations:
"Peace upon Moses and Aaron."
Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
Indeed, they were of Our believing servants.
And indeed, Elias was from among the messengers,
When he said to his people, "Will you not fear Allah?
Do you call upon Ba'l and leave the best of creators -
1.transliteration: Atad'oona Ba'lanw wa tazaroona ahsanal khaaliqeen

1.english: Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers?"
1.transliteration: Allaaha Rabbakum wa Rabba aabaaa'ikumul awwaleen

1.english: And they denied him, so indeed, they will be brought [for punishment],
Except the chosen servants of Allah.
And We left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:
"Peace upon Elias."
Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.
And indeed, Lot was among the messengers.
[So mention] when We saved him and his family, all,
Except his wife among those who remained [with the evildoers].
Then We destroyed the others.
And indeed, you pass by them in the morning
And at night. Then will you not use reason?
And indeed, Jonah was among the messengers.
[Mention] when he ran away to the laden ship.
1.transliteration: Iz abaqa ilal fulkil mash hoon

1.english: And he drew lots and was among the losers.
Then the fish swallowed him, while he was blameworthy.
And had he not been of those who exalt Allah,
1.transliteration: Falaw laaa annahoo kaana minal musabbiheen

1.arabic: للبث في بطنه إلى يوم يبعثون
1.english: He would have remained inside its belly until the Day they are resurrected.
1.transliteration: Lalabisa fee batniheee ilaa Yawmi yub'asoon

1.english: But We threw him onto the open shore while he was ill.
And We caused to grow over him a gourd vine.

1.arabic: وأرسلناه إلى مائة ألف أو يزيدون
1.english: And We sent him to [his people of] a hundred thousand or more.
1.transliteration: Wa arsalnaahu ilaa mi'ati alfin aw yazeedoon

1.english: And they believed, so We gave them enjoyment [of life] for a time.
1.transliteration: Fa aamanoo famatta' naahum ilaa heen

1.english: So inquire of them, [O Muhammad], "Does your Lord have daughters while they have sons?
1.transliteration: Fastaftihim ali Rabbikal banaatu wa lahumul banoon

1.arabic: أم خلقنا الملائكة إناثا وهم شاهدون
1.english: Or did We create the angels as females while they were witnesses?"
1.transliteration: Am khalaqnal malaaa'i kata inaasanw wa hm shaahidoon

1.english: Unquestionably, it is out of their [invented] falsehood that they say,
1.transliteration: Alaaa innahum min ifkihim la yaqooloon

1.english: " Allah has begotten," and indeed, they are liars.
Has He chosen daughters over sons?
What is [wrong] with you? How do you make judgement?
Then will you not be reminded?
Or do you have a clear authority?
Then produce your scripture, if you should be truthful.
1.transliteration: Faatoo bi Kitaabikum in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: وجعلوا بينه وبين الجنة نسبا ۚ ولقد علمت الجنة إنهم لمحضرون
1.english: And they have claimed between Him and the jinn a lineage, but the jinn have already known that they [who made such claims] will be brought to [punishment].
1.transliteration: Wa ja'aloo bainahoo wa bainal jinnati nasabaa; wa laqad 'alimatil jinnatu innahum lamuhdaroon

1.english: Exalted is Allah above what they describe,
Except the chosen servants of Allah [who do not share in that sin].
So indeed, you [disbelievers] and whatever you worship,
You cannot tempt [anyone] away from Him
Except he who is to [enter and] burn in the Hellfire.

1.arabic: وما منا إلا له مقام معلوم
1.english: [The angels say], "There is not among us any except that he has a known position.
1.transliteration: Wa maa minnasa illaa lahoo maqaamum ma'loom

1.english: And indeed, we are those who line up [for prayer].
And indeed, we are those who exalt Allah."
And indeed, the disbelievers used to say,

1.arabic: لو أن عندنا ذكرا من الأولين
1.english: "If we had a message from [those of] the former peoples,
We would have been the chosen servants of Allah."
But they disbelieved in it, so they are going to know.
And Our word has already preceded for Our servants, the messengers,
1.transliteration: Wa laqad sabaqat Kalimatunaa li'ibaadinal mursa leen

1.english: [That] indeed, they would be those given victory
And [that] indeed, Our soldiers will be those who overcome.
So, [O Muhammad], leave them for a time.
And see [what will befall] them, for they are going to see.
Then for Our punishment are they impatient?

1.arabic: فإذا نزل بساحتهم فساء صباح المنذرين
1.english: But when it descends in their territory, then evil is the morning of those who were warned.
1.transliteration: Fa izaa nazala bisaahatihim fasaaa'a sabaahul munzareen

1.english: And leave them for a time.
And see, for they are going to see.

1.arabic: سبحان ربك رب العزة عما يصفون
1.english: Exalted is your Lord, the Lord of might, above what they describe.
And peace upon the messengers.
And praise to Allah, Lord of the worlds.
